location.cost_of_living_band: Moderate for a student suburb; hostel/PG and lifestyle dependent
location.data_origin: Address and major connectivity official; coordinates, local distances and travel time map-derived; cost band inferred

senior_junior_culture.summary: Recent public discussions commonly describe seniors as accessible through clubs, AMAs and hostel networks, with no repeated strong ragging signal in the reviewed sample. Evidence is still self-reported and insufficient for a campus-wide guarantee.
positive_themes: AMAs and club communities provide informal help
Freshers can access senior networks through technical and cultural clubs
negative_themes: Mentorship quality depends on the club and personal network
Absence of repeated reports is not proof that incidents never occur

academic_culture.summary: The most repeated theme is a structured system with a 75% attendance expectation and a very large cohort. Students report substantial opportunities, but faculty accessibility and teaching quality vary by department and instructor, making self-directed learning important.
positive_themes: Large elective, lab and project ecosystem
Technical clubs and hackathons supplement classroom learning
negative_themes: Attendance is frequently described as strict
Large classes and faculty variability are recurring concerns

hostel_reality.summary: Hostel experience is strongly block-dependent. Officially published room categories vary from low-cost common-bathroom sharing to premium AC attached rooms, including some off-campus options. Students repeatedly report that popular rooms fill quickly, girls’ rules can feel stricter, and mess opinions differ.
positive_themes: Wide choice of room occupancy and AC/non-AC formats
Many options are integrated with the campus
Hostel life improves access to evening events and peer groups
negative_themes: Current curfew was not officially verified
hostel_reality.source_agreement: Consistent on variation and booking pressure; conflicting on mess quality

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official pages show a broad cultural, service, sports and technical ecosystem, and student sources consistently describe frequent events and hackathons. With a very large student population, selection and leadership opportunities can be competitive.
positive_themes: Hackathons and ideathons provide project exposure
negative_themes: Static official lists do not prove every club is active
Recruitment and leadership roles can be competitive

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: The institution has visible technical clubs, hackathons and a broad recruiter ecosystem. Official NIRF placement counts are strong at engineering-institution level, while student reports stress intense competition created by the large intake and uncertainty about branch/company eligibility. Kattankulathur-only outcomes are not separately proven.
positive_themes: Large recruiter list and high NIRF placed counts
Multiple CSE specialisations and peer communities
negative_themes: Branch-wise and KTR-only placement transparency is limited
Non-CSE access to software roles is company-dependent
coding_and_placement_culture.source_agreement: Opportunity is consistent; outcome interpretation varies

locality_and_student_life.summary: Kattankulathur/Potheri is a campus-oriented student suburb with suburban-rail access and an on-campus hospital. Central Chennai is roughly 40 km away, so city internships and social access often require train, bus or longer road travel.
positive_themes: Large campus provides daily services and healthcare
Nearby student housing and food ecosystem
negative_themes: Peak traffic can make airport and city travel slow

overall_satisfaction.summary: The most repeated positives are infrastructure, choice of programmes, clubs and recruiter breadth. The most repeated concerns are high cost, very large intake, attendance/hostel rules and uneven branch-level transparency. Outcomes appear especially dependent on branch, personal initiative and scholarship affordability.
positive_themes: Strong institutional placement reach at aggregate level
negative_themes: High combined tuition and hostel cost
No public official branch closing ranks or KTR-only placement sheet
who_it_appears_best_suited_for: Self-driven students who will use clubs, projects and internships
Students comfortable with a large campus and cohort
Families able to afford the full fee without assuming scholarship renewal
who_may_not_enjoy_it: Students wanting small classes and highly personalised administration
Students seeking a low-cost programme with branch-wise ROI transparency
Students strongly opposed to attendance or hostel restrictions
overall_satisfaction.would_choose_again_evidence: Mixed and not supported by a sufficiently large independent sample
overall_satisfaction.source_agreement: Positive opportunity themes agree; value and outcome judgments vary

parent_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
parent_mode_summary.total_cost: Published annual tuition is INR 2.75–4.75 lakh. A calculated first-year institutional range using the lowest/highest published tuition and sampled hostel/mess totals is roughly INR 3.95–7.58 lakh, excluding personal costs.
parent_mode_summary.scholarship_possibilities: Substantial rank-based and category scholarships exist, but allocations are phase-specific and conditional. Eligibility appears to match only after the official award letter.
parent_mode_summary.placement_stability: NIRF engineering data show 92–96% placed relative to graduating students for the latest three years, but the submission is not proven Kattankulathur-only and branch-level outcomes are unavailable.
parent_mode_summary.travel_and_medical: Potheri rail access is excellent and an SRM hospital is on/adjacent to campus; central Chennai and the airport require longer travel.
parent_mode_summary.hostel: Many price levels exist, including off-campus options. Preferred blocks are not guaranteed.
parent_mode_summary.academic_discipline: Student sources repeatedly describe strict attendance; verify current academic rules.

student_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
student_mode_summary.branch_fit: Compare the exact specialisation curriculum and placement pool rather than treating all CSE labels as equivalent.
student_mode_summary.coding_and_core_culture: Hackathons, coding communities and broad technical activity are visible, but initiative is important in a large cohort.
student_mode_summary.clubs_and_campus_life: Campus life is a major strength, with many technical, cultural and sports options; selection can be competitive.
student_mode_summary.academic_flexibility: Opportunities are broad, while attendance and faculty experience may constrain flexibility.
student_mode_summary.hostel_experience: Room/block choice changes cost, privacy, washroom type, rules and commute; some official boys’ options are off-campus.
student_mode_summary.city_exposure: Potheri is self-contained and rail-connected, but central Chennai opportunities require commuting.
student_mode_summary.placement_roadmap: Begin fundamentals and projects early, join a technical community, seek internships by second/third year, and verify company eligibility for the exact branch.

decision_risks.evidence: Annual tuition is INR 2.75–4.75 lakh; sampled first-year hostel/mess totals are INR 1.10–2.73 lakh, before personal expenses.
decision_risks.mitigation: Model affordability without assuming scholarship, future fee stability or the cheapest room.

decision_risks.risk: No public official branch closing ranks
decision_risks.evidence: No completed-year branch-wise SRMJEEE closing-rank table meeting import requirements was located.
decision_risks.mitigation: Obtain an official archived allotment export before cutoff prediction.

decision_risks.evidence: The current institution page totals 5,760 Kattankulathur seats across 44 programmes.
decision_risks.mitigation: Assess class size, internal competition, specialisation quality and branch placement access.

decision_risks.evidence: NIRF and recruiter claims may include broader engineering/institution/group scope.
decision_risks.mitigation: Request campus- and branch-specific eligible, registered and placed counts.

decision_risks.evidence: Rank bands are phase-specific and awards are limited/conditional.
decision_risks.mitigation: Treat scholarship as upside, not a guaranteed budget assumption.

decision_risks.evidence: Official options span on-campus and off-campus blocks, and student reports describe fast booking.
decision_risks.mitigation: Budget for a higher-cost fallback and verify location/rules before payment.

decision_risks.evidence: Recent student sources repeatedly describe strict attendance and residential rules.
decision_risks.mitigation: Read current academic and hostel regulations before choosing a residential programme.

decision_risks.evidence: Many specialisations are offered, but company-wise eligible branches are not publicly mapped.
decision_risks.mitigation: Verify curriculum, common placement pool and company eligibility for the exact programme code.

decision_risks.evidence: Campus is around 40 km from central Chennai, despite good suburban-rail access.
decision_risks.mitigation: Plan commute time for external internships, events and airport travel.

decision_risks.risk: Core branch selected for software-only goal
decision_risks.evidence: Official recruiter pages do not publish universal software eligibility for every branch.
decision_risks.mitigation: Choose the core curriculum only if acceptable and maintain a dual core/software preparation plan.
